scored_papers.title: Eigenstate-Selective Entangled Two-Photon Absorption in Monolayer WSe$_2$
scored_papers.abstract: We show that the Bell-state phase of a polarization-entangled photon pair controls the biexciton eigenstate distribution produced by entangled two-photon absorption (ETPA) in monolayer WSe$_2$. In a frequency-nondegenerate ladder scheme, two independent valley pathways ($K$ and $K'$) share no intermediate state, so the biphoton phase sets the relative amplitude between them. Within the valley-symmetric limit this phase factorizes from the material response, and the resulting selection rule partitions the excitation among biexciton eigenstates according to the Bell-state phase $\varphi$. The symmetric Bell state ($\varphi = 0$) selectively drives bright eigenstates, while the antisymmetric state ($\varphi = \pi$) drives the exchange-dark eigenstate. No classical polarization source reproduces this $\varphi$-dependent eigenstate distribution. Including valley dephasing and intervalley scattering at 4~K, the phase-scan visibility exceeds $0.97$ for broadband SPDC ($T_e \sim 100$~fs) with high source purity.
scored_papers.reason: 偏光もつれ光子対の Bell 相が biexciton 固有状態の選択則を決定✨ ETPA + SPDC ど真ん中の必読論文🎯
scored_papers.summary: K/K′ 二谷経路で振幅比が biphoton 位相に係留📐 φ=0 で bright, φ=π で exchange-dark を選択、4K でも可視度 0.97 超
